title: BBC Wales: A465 Heads of the Valleys Road Closed by Flooding
evidence: BBC Wales reports full closure of the A465 between Brynmawr and Clydach. Reports include interview with Traffic Wales spokesperson confirming flood depth and no reopening timeline. Article notes Environment Agency flood warning for Clydach Gorge. First major news coverage of the event.

assessment.justification: BBC Wales report corroborates Traffic Wales official closure. Spokesperson quote adds credibility. First major news milestone of the event. Strong signal corroboration.
assessment.synthesis: BBC Wales confirming A465 closure with Traffic Wales spokesperson. Marks the event breaking into mainstream news coverage. Strong corroboration of the primary SE Wales road closure.
assessment.confidenceStatement: High confidence. BBC Wales with official spokesperson quote. Strongly corroborates Traffic Wales closure data.

title: WalesOnline: Abergavenny Flooding – River Usk Levels Critical
evidence: WalesOnline publishes report on Abergavenny flooding with reference to River Usk approaching critical levels. Article includes photos from residents and quotes from Natural Resources Wales. Describes A40 as 'partially impassable' and warns of further deterioration.

assessment.justification: WalesOnline report citing Natural Resources Wales and featuring resident photos. River Usk levels described as critical, consistent with upstream gauge data and hydrological modelling. Adds community dimension to official reporting.
assessment.synthesis: WalesOnline reporting on Abergavenny flood conditions with Natural Resources Wales input. River Usk levels critical. Corroborates official data and adds community impact narrative.
assessment.confidenceStatement: Medium confidence. Credible reporting with official agency input and photographic evidence.

title: BBC News: M4 Motorway Closed – Chaos on South Wales Routes
evidence: BBC News website publishes national-level article on the M4 motorway closure between J24 and J25 Newport. Article describes the closure as 'one of the most significant transport disruptions in South Wales in recent years'. References Transport for Wales rail cancellations and growing queues on diversion routes.

assessment.justification: BBC News national coverage strongly corroborates the M4 closure and its strategic significance. Media characterisation of the event as historically significant is consistent with the dual road-rail closure picture emerging from official sources.
assessment.synthesis: BBC News providing national-level coverage of the M4 Newport closure. Characterises the event as historically significant for South Wales transport. Strongly corroborates strategic impact of the closure.
assessment.confidenceStatement: High confidence. National BBC News coverage with cross-corroboration from multiple official transport sources.

title: Hereford Times: Wye Flooding Threatens A40 Near Ross
evidence: Hereford Times online reports River Wye flooding threatening the A40 near Ross-on-Wye. Environment Agency gauge data quoted showing 4.2m and rising. Article notes single-lane working on the A40 and warns of potential full closure. Quotes from local residents.

assessment.justification: Regional news source quoting EA gauge data. Consistent with National Highways advisory for A40 at Ross-on-Wye. Adds Herefordshire dimension to the impact picture.
assessment.synthesis: Hereford Times reporting on River Wye conditions and A40 threat near Ross-on-Wye. Consistent with official EA gauge data and National Highways advisory. Adds Herefordshire regional media corroboration.
assessment.confidenceStatement: Medium confidence. Regional media with EA gauge data reference. Corroborates official sources.

title: Gloucestershire Live: A417 Disruption and M4 Diversion Chaos
evidence: Gloucestershire Live publishes article on A417 surface water disruption near Cirencester and reports of unusual congestion across the county due to traffic diverted from the M4 closure. Notes HGV aquaplaning incident and single-lane running. Quotes from commuters stuck in queues.

assessment.justification: Gloucestershire Live reporting consistent with National Highways A417 data and expected diversion impact from M4 closure. Adds regional media corroboration for the Gloucestershire secondary impact zone.
assessment.synthesis: Gloucestershire Live covering A417 disruption and M4 diversion impact across the county. Consistent with official data. Corroborates Gloucestershire as a significant secondary impact area.
assessment.confidenceStatement: Medium confidence. Regional media corroborates A417 and M4 diversion impacts in Gloucestershire.

title: WalesOnline: Newport Flooding – Community Impact and Emergency Response
evidence: WalesOnline publishes comprehensive article on Newport flooding, residential impacts, and emergency service response. Notes properties flooded near River Usk, emergency shelters opened, and South Wales Police advice to avoid travel through Newport entirely. Describes it as 'the worst flooding Newport has seen in over a decade'.

assessment.justification: WalesOnline comprehensive community impact article with police attribution. Emergency shelter opening is a strong indicator of significant residential impact. 'Worst in a decade' characterisation is consistent with the scale of road and rail closures.
assessment.synthesis: WalesOnline comprehensive coverage of Newport's flooding emergency with police attribution and emergency shelter confirmation. Supports the assessment of this as a significant, high-impact event for Southeast Wales.
assessment.confidenceStatement: High confidence. Confirmed emergency shelters opened and South Wales Police attribution. Multiple official sources corroborate severity.

title: Worcestershire Live: Surface Water Causing Disruption Across County
locationName: Worcestershire | Vale of Evesham | Mid Worcestershire
evidence: Worcestershire Live publishes a rolling traffic update noting surface water disruption on multiple Worcestershire roads including the A44, A422, and several B-roads across the Vale of Evesham. Highways England and Worcestershire County Council advisories cited. Advice to check before travelling issued to motorists.

assessment.justification: Worcestershire Live aggregating official highway advisories for the county. Consistent with the pattern of surface water disruption spreading into central England. Regional media amplification of official source data.
assessment.synthesis: Worcestershire Live covering surface water disruption across the county. Media amplification of official advisories. Adds regional news corroboration to the central England minor impact picture.
assessment.confidenceStatement: Medium confidence. Regional media aggregating official advisories. Consistent with conditions.

title: Newbury Weekly News: A34 and Local Roads Affected by Surface Water
evidence: Newbury Weekly News online publishes a traffic advisory alert noting surface water on the A34 and on local roads across West Berkshire. Article references National Highways advisory and quotes Berkshire emergency planning team as monitoring the situation. Thames Water also noted as monitoring the River Kennet.

assessment.justification: Local media coverage in the Thames Valley consistent with National Highways A34 advisory. Multi-agency monitoring (Berkshire emergency planning, Thames Water) adds credibility to the assessment that conditions in the area are being actively watched.
assessment.synthesis: Newbury local news reporting surface water on A34 and local roads with multi-agency monitoring noted. Adds Thames Valley regional media context to the central England minor impact picture.
assessment.confidenceStatement: Medium confidence. Local media corroborating official advisory. Multi-agency monitoring mentioned.
